title: Middle East war: Gulf economies on the brink of suffocation
subhead: Although the six Gulf countries have diversified their economies, hydrocarbons still account for about 30% of their gross domestic product (GDP). The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is also preventing them from importing goods, while the region relies on imports for 85% of its food supply.

title: Why is Kharg Island, Iran's key oil export hub, so important?
subhead: Tehran threatened to 'destroy and turn into a pile of ashes' US energy and oil infrastructure in the region after US strikes on the key island on Friday.

title: Zelensky and European leaders warn the US against easing sanctions on Russia
subhead: During a visit to Paris, the Ukrainian president criticized the partial lifting of sanctions on Russian oil. The measure, decided by the US administration in response to soaring prices caused by the war in Iran, strengthens Moscow's position in Ukraine.

title: Races to watch in France's 2026 municipal elections
subhead: Aside from major cities like Paris and Marseille, the local election results from smaller towns like Le Havre and Menton are expected to reveal early political trends ahead of the 2027 presidential and legislative contests.

title: Citizen investigators dig into the Epstein files
subhead: Since the declassification of the Epstein files, countless internet users have been delving into this massive trove of emails, videos, official reports and personal diaries. This citizen investigation sometimes turns into obsession.

'In 1979, the Iranian left played a major role in the rise of the Islamic Republic'
subhead: Philosopher Stéphanie Roza and political scientist Amirpasha Tavakkoli explain how, throughout the 20th century, the political turmoil in Iran, torn between modernity and anti-modernity, ultimately led to Islamist victory.

title: Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
subhead: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.

title: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
subhead: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.
